comment.narrative: The staff recruitment was not completed, and the activity is not done.
The staff recruitment was not completed, and the activity is not done.

In this reporting period, MSD created demand on case management services and 3 cases were identified ( 1 survivor of GBV and 2 neglected from education and health) and 3 of them were linked to the service in collaboration with consortium partner PAD. The

Community based child protection committee(CBCPC) was established in the three targeted Kebeles comprises of community leaders, women, religious leaders. Now we are planning to provide training by the 3rd week of July 2024

In this quarter, MSD identified 300HH for MPC with the presence of stakeholder and beneficiaries were verified and complaints were properly handled by the compliant committee. and beneficiaries accounts were documented. All in all the process went well.

300 household beneficiaries already reported in the 3rd Quarter.

Not planned for this year
This is an emergency report. We provided MPC to 366 households in Dhana Woreda as part of the crisis modifier response.
Multi-purpose cash assistance was provided to 300 households, including 17 beneficiaries who had also received MPC support in the previous year.
Multi-purpose cash assistance was provided to 300 households, including 17 beneficiaries who had also received MPC support in the previous year.

with the EJR partners in Ziquala project area, MSD were organized a joint launching workshop at Seqota

Joint supportive Supervision (JSS) was organized with other EJR partners with the government. Few number of government representative participated.
Joint supportive Supervision (JSS) was organized with other EJR partners with the government. Few number of government representative participated.
Project staff and the Woreda Disaster Prevention Officer participated in the data verification and monitoring of the beneficiary selection activity.
Project staff and the Woreda Disaster Prevention Officer participated in the data verification and monitoring of the beneficiary selection activity.

Discussion was conducted with staff from Women and Children's Social Affairs office staffs, One stop center in Ziquala hospital to understand emerging needs in the revision of the project to adapt implementation.

Discussion was conducted with staff from Women and Children's Social Affairs office staffs, One stop center in Ziquala hospital to understand emerging needs in the revision of the project to adapt implementation.
Among the participants from our stakeholders who took part in the planning and monitoring activities, 17 were repeat participants from the CCC training and Referral Pathway workshop.

The beneficiaries for IPA and alternative care identified but only supported 54 people. Others opening of bank account for IPA and purchase of materials for alternative care is on process.
The beneficiaries for IPA and alternative care identified but only supported 54 people. Others opening of bank account for IPA and purchase of materials for alternative care is on process.

This result was achieved through close collaboration with community structures to identify vulnerable children in the community, which significantly increased the number of children included in case management services and those receiving IPA support.

This result was achieved through close collaboration with community structures to identify vulnerable children in the community, which significantly increased the number of children included in case management services and those receiving IPA support.

The cost incurred is lowered than the plan because of the sessions are conducted in rural areas where halls available for free.

Working closely with CBCPC members, CRCs and collaborating with other stakeholders has produced this result.
